pattern: DP on inversion count with prefix constraints: dp[i][j] = count of permutations of [0..i] with exactly j inversions, using a sliding-window prefix sum; requirements zero out the DP except at the pinned count.
hints: When inserting element i into a permutation of [0..i-1], placing it in position p from the right adds p new inversions. So dp[i][j] sums dp[i-1][j-p] for p = 0..i — a window sum computable in O(max_cnt) per row.
Each requirement [end, cnt] forces dp[end][cnt] to be the only non-zero entry at position end. Carry only that value forward; if it would be zero, the answer is 0.
Use a sliding-window prefix sum to compute each DP row in O(max_cnt) time. Total: O(n * max_cnt). The answer is dp[n-1][req_map[n-1]].

edge_inputs.description: example 2: n=3, three requirements pinning all
example 3: n=2, no inversions at both positions
single requirement at n-1 with 0 inversions (only sorted perm)
requirement at position 0 with cnt=0 (only one starting perm)
impossible: cnt exceeds max inversions for that prefix
n=5, middle constraint at position 2 with cnt=2

stress.description: small n with random requirements (oracle uses permutations so n<=7)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, size)
    indices = list(range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, min(n, 3))
    ends = sorted(rng.sample(indices, k))
    if n - 1 not in ends:
        ends[-1] = n - 1
    ends = sorted(set(ends))
    max_inv = n * (n - 1) // 2
    reqs = [[e, rng.randint(0, e * (e + 1) // 2)] for e in ends]
    return [n, reqs]
